description: Add archived column to OrchestratorDescriptor, create Alembic migration, update repository with archive filtering, add archive/unarchive UI endpoints, update templates with archive/unarchive buttons and archived releases view, block execution/schedule listing for archived releases, and add tests.
steps: 1. Add `archived` Boolean column to OrchestratorDescriptor model in `src/maestro/database/models.py`. Use `Column(Integer, nullable=False, server_default='0')` (Integer 0/1 for SQLite compatibility, works as boolean). Add it after `created_at`.

2. Create Alembic migration file `migrations/versions/f6a7b8c9d0e1_add_archived_to_orchestrator_descriptor.py` with revision='f6a7b8c9d0e1' and down_revision='e5f6a7b8c9d0'. Use `op.add_column('orchestrator_descriptor', sa.Column('archived', sa.Integer(), nullable=False, server_default='0'))` in upgrade and `op.drop_column('orchestrator_descriptor', 'archived')` in downgrade. Use `render_as_batch=True` pattern is NOT needed here since add_column works fine without batch mode. Follow the exact pattern of existing migrations (imports, typing hints, docstring).

3. Update `src/maestro/repositories/orchestrator.py`: Add an `archived` parameter (default False) to `get_all()` and `get_count()`. When `archived=False`, filter by `OrchestratorDescriptor.archived == 0`. When `archived=True`, filter by `OrchestratorDescriptor.archived == 1`. Add a new method `set_archived(descriptor_id: int, archived: bool) -> OrchestratorDescriptor | None` that fetches the descriptor by id and sets its archived field to 1 or 0, commits, and returns the updated descriptor.

4. Update `src/maestro/api/routes/ui.py`: (a) Modify `partials_releases` endpoint to pass `archived=False` to `orchestrator_repo.get_all()` and `orchestrator_repo.get_count()` so the main releases page only shows non-archived releases. (b) Add a new endpoint `POST /ui/releases/{descriptor_id}/archive` that calls `orchestrator_repo.set_archived(descriptor_id, True)` and returns a partial HTML response (HX-Trigger: refreshReleases). (c) Add a new endpoint `POST /ui/releases/{descriptor_id}/unarchive` that calls `orchestrator_repo.set_archived(descriptor_id, False)` and returns a partial HTML response (HX-Trigger: refreshReleases). (d) Add a new endpoint `GET /ui/releases/archived` that renders a new template `releases_archived.html`. (e) Add a new endpoint `GET /ui/partials/releases-archived` that fetches archived releases (archived=True) with pagination/search and renders `partials/releases_archived_table.html`. (f) In `partials_executions` endpoint, join with OrchestratorDescriptor to exclude executions belonging to archived releases (or add filtering logic in the execution repository). (g) Block the execute and schedule endpoints for archived releases: in `execute_release_ui`, before calling orchestrator_service.execute_release, check if the descriptor is archived. In `schedule_release_ui`, similarly check. Return an error HTML partial if archived.

5. Create template `src/maestro/ui/templates/releases_archived.html` following the same pattern as `releases.html` but with title 'Releases Arquivadas', no upload section, and loading the archived partials table via `hx-get='/ui/partials/releases-archived'`.

6. Create template `src/maestro/ui/templates/partials/releases_archived_table.html` following the same pattern as `partials/releases_table.html` but without execute/schedule/dry-run buttons. Instead, show an 'Unarchive' button for each release that does `hx-post='/ui/releases/{desc.id}/unarchive'` with hx-target='#releases-archived-table-container' and appropriate hx-swap. The container div id should be `releases-archived-table-container`.

7. Update `src/maestro/ui/templates/partials/releases_table.html`: Add an 'Archive' button (small, ghost/outline style with an archive icon) to the actions column for each release. The button should do `hx-post='/ui/releases/{desc.id}/archive'` with `hx-confirm='Arquivar a release {{ desc.name }}?'` and trigger `refreshReleases` on success. Place it after the existing YAML button.

8. Update `src/maestro/ui/templates/base.html`: Add a new sidebar menu item 'Arquivadas' (or 'Releases Arquivadas') linking to `/ui/releases/archived`, placed after the 'Releases' item. Use an archive-style SVG icon.

9. Add tests in `tests/test_archive_releases.py`: (a) Test `OrchestratorDescriptorRepository.get_all(archived=False)` filters out archived descriptors. (b) Test `OrchestratorDescriptorRepository.get_all(archived=True)` returns only archived descriptors. (c) Test `OrchestratorDescriptorRepository.set_archived()` updates the field. (d) Test POST `/ui/releases/1/archive` endpoint returns 200. (e) Test POST `/ui/releases/1/unarchive` endpoint returns 200. (f) Test GET `/ui/releases/archived` returns 200 HTML. (g) Test that execute endpoint for archived release returns error. Follow the patterns in `tests/test_ui_routes.py` and `tests/test_repositories.py`.
acceptance_criteria: OrchestratorDescriptor model has an `archived` Integer column with server_default='0'
Alembic migration file exists and adds the archived column
POST /ui/releases/{id}/archive sets archived=1 and returns success
POST /ui/releases/{id}/unarchive sets archived=0 and returns success
GET /ui/releases/archived renders the archived releases page
Execute and schedule endpoints reject archived releases with an error message
Archive button appears in the releases table for each release
Unarchive button appears in the archived releases table
Sidebar has a link to the archived releases page
All new tests pass with `pytest tests/test_archive_releases.py -x -q`
verification: Run `cd /projects/sandbox/maestro && uv run pytest tests/test_archive_releases.py -x -q` and confirm all tests pass
Run `cd /projects/sandbox/maestro && uv run pytest tests/ -x --ignore=tests/integration -q` and confirm existing tests still pass
Run `cd /projects/sandbox/maestro && uv run python -c 'from maestro.database.models import OrchestratorDescriptor; print(OrchestratorDescriptor.archived)'` and confirm no import errors
